prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A wooden desk in what looks like a 1960s small-town Indian newspaper office. A vintage typewriter sits center, a half-typed letter still loaded in it. A small stack of newspapers and a brass paperweight beside it. A tilted desk lamp at the upper right casts warm amber light over the scene. Dark wood-paneled background, loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain. No text other than indistinct typewriter print.
alt: An old-fashioned wooden desk in a newspaper office, a half-typed letter still in the typewriter, soft golden lamp light

prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A writer's desk in late afternoon light. alt: Painterly scene of a writer at a wooden desk in late afternoon light, books and lined notebooks visible, soft window light

prompt: Watercolour painting — an ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5). A 1908 South Indian writing scene: a man in traditional turban and thick black moustache, seated cross-legged at a low writing desk, writing with a fountain pen on loose sheets of paper. An oil lamp glows beside him. The room is sparse — a thin mat on the floor, a faint wall calendar marked 1908. Warm amber lamp light, dark wooden background, loose watercolour washes, soft wet-on-wet colour bleeds, granulation and visible paper grain. Dignified, focused expression. No text other than the indistinct calendar year.
alt: A painterly scene from around 1908: a man in a turban and thick moustache writing by oil lamp at a low desk, the year visible on a calendar nearby
